In this lesson, we're going to talk about what to focus your testing on and why. Now, I first heard my friend and mentor, Michael Masterson, or by his real name, Mark Ford, say something as it relates to testing that has stayed with me ever since. He said, you want to focus on the things that scream, not on the things that whisper. What he was talking about was focusing on the big elements of your marketing funnel, the things that have the ability to really make an impact, not the little things that only have the ability to make a little impact. There are three main things that you want to focus your testing on. The first one is the offer. You've already heard me say that the offer is far more important than the copy, than the messaging, than what you say. It's far more important than how you lay out the slides on your VSL or the color of the background on your webpage or the color of your call to action button or whether your button says instant access or free instant access or get instant access. See, testing the offer has the ability to impact sales in a big way. That's the first thing that I want you to focus your testing on. When you start testing, you start testing the offer. You start testing how the offer is presented. You start testing the price point. You start testing with the premiums, the guarantee. You start testing with whether there's a payment plan or not, whether it's a soft offer or a hard offer. You start by testing the offer. The next is the headline. Of course, when it comes to the copy, it all begins with the headline. When it comes to a VSL, the headline becomes the hook. It's testing the hook, testing a different hook, testing a different hook and promise. The next one is the big idea or the premise. You may have designed this hook, let's say going back to the common example that we've used throughout the Six Figure Funnel Formula Training is this idea of if you're 54 years or older and you're behind in your retirement, well, then there is a new investment vehicle that could double your net worth in five years. That's the idea. The idea is that there is this new investment vehicle that can double your net worth. Maybe there's a different idea or different premise that we can present to individuals over the age of 54 that is not based on doubling their net worth. Maybe it is there's a new investment vehicle that will save you from living an impoverished lifestyle in your golden years. That's a different idea. That's a different premise. You are testing the offer, the headline or the hook and you're testing the idea, the premise behind your marketing. When you start testing, your testing should begin in the order of priority that you see right here on this page. It's testing these elements and testing these elements really in this order that have the potential to significantly increase sales conversions. Changing the offer, tweaking the offer can sometimes double the response rate, double the amount of money that you are generating. Whereas changing the color of a button on your page doesn't have the potential to have that same impact. Changing the background color on a web page, for example, isn't going to double your response rates. Certainly changing the offer, changing the positioning of the offer, changing the hook, changing the overall idea or premise behind your marketing has the power to have a massive impact on your conversions. These are the things that you should focus your testing on.
